perturbation.original_text: Each of Stockholders severally agrees that such Stockholder shall not, at any time following the effectiveness of the Merger, print, publish, divulge or communicate to any person, firm, corporation or other business organization or use for his or their own account any business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, product designs and specifications, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, promotional materials or other secret or confidential information relating to the Business or any other businesses then being engaged in by AUGI or any of its subsidiaries, or any secret or confidential information relating to the affairs, dealings and concerns of the Company, AUGI or any of the subsidiaries of AUGI (collectively, the "Confidential Information") which the Stockholder has received or obtained while an employee or member of the Board of Directors of AUGI or the Company (whether or not developed, devised, or otherwise created in whole or in part by the efforts of the Stockholder).
perturbation.changed_text: Each of Stockholders severally agrees that such Stockholder shall use reasonable discretion to not, at any time following the effectiveness of the Merger, print, publish, divulge or communicate to unauthorized individuals or entities any business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, product designs and specifications, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, promotional materials or other secret or confidential information relating to the Business or any other businesses then being engaged in by AUGI or any of its subsidiaries, or any secret or confidential information relating to the affairs, dealings and concerns of the Company, AUGI or any of the subsidiaries of AUGI (collectively, the "Confidential Information") which the Stockholder has received or obtained while an employee or member of the Board of Directors of AUGI or the Company (whether or not developed, devised, or otherwise created in whole or in part by the efforts of the Stockholder).
perturbation.explanation: Adding "use reasonable discretion to not" and "unauthorized individuals or entities" introduces ambiguity. 'Reasonable discretion' is subjective, and it's unclear who determines which individuals/entities are 'unauthorized'. This makes enforcement of the confidentiality clause uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Delaware Uniform Trade Secrets Act (DUTSA) 6 Del. C. § 2001
perturbation.law_explanation: The altered text contradicts the Delaware Uniform Trade Secrets Act (DUTSA) by weakening the protection of trade secrets. DUTSA requires reasonable efforts to maintain the secrecy of information. The phrase "use reasonable discretion to not" provides a loophole, making it harder to prove that a stockholder failed to take appropriate measures to protect confidential information, especially if they claim their 'discretion' led them to believe the disclosure was permissible or to an authorized party. This ambiguity directly undermines the 'reasonable efforts' standard required to claim trade secret protection under DUTSA.

Derives independent economic value, actual or potential, from not being generally known to, and not b
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces ambiguity that weakens the protection of trade secrets, potentially contradicting the spirit of the Delaware Uniform Trade Secrets Act (DUTSA). The addition of "use reasonable discretion to not" and limiting disclosure to "unauthorized individuals or entities" creates a loophole. While the provided snippet doesn't explicitly use the phrase 'reasonable efforts', it defines 'Misappropriation' as disclosure or use of a trade secret 'without express or implied consent'. The modified text makes it harder to prove misappropriation because a stockholder could argue their 'reasonable discretion' led them to believe the disclosure was permissible or to an authorized party, thereby implying consent. This undermines the secrecy and protection that DUTSA aims to provide, especially concerning 'information' that 'Derives independent economic value' as per the definition of 'Trade secret'.

perturbation.original_text: Any paragraph, sentence, phrase or other provision of this Agreement which is in conflict with any applicable statute, rule or other law shall be deemed, if possible, to be modified or altered to conform thereto or, if not possible, to be omitted herefrom.
perturbation.changed_text: Any paragraph, sentence, phrase or other provision of this Agreement which appears to be in conflict with any potentially applicable statute, rule or other law may be subject to modification or alteration to generally conform thereto or, if not feasible, it could be omitted herefrom.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "is in conflict" to "appears to be in conflict", "applicable statute, rule or other law" to "potentially applicable statute, rule or other law," "shall be deemed, if possible, to be modified or altered" to "may be subject to modification or alteration", "to conform thereto" to "to generally conform thereto", and "if not possible" to "if not feasible" introduces subjectivity and discretion. It is now unclear who determines if something 'appears' to be in conflict, what laws are 'potentially applicable', and what constitutes 'general conformity' or 'feasibility.'
perturbation.contradicted_law: Principles of Contract Law in Delaware
perturbation.law_citation: See e.g., Nemec v. Shrader, 991 A.2d 1120, 1125-26 (Del. 2010)
perturbation.law_explanation: Delaware law, as illustrated in cases like *Nemec v. Shrader*, emphasizes the importance of clear and unambiguous contractual language. The modifications create excessive ambiguity in the severability clause, undermining the predictability and enforceability that Delaware law seeks to uphold. The new language provides substantial room for interpretation, potentially leading to disputes over which provisions are invalid and how they should be modified. This contradicts the principle that contracts should be interpreted to give effect to the parties' intent, as the ambiguity obscures the original intent regarding severability.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Delaware Code Online Title 6 > Chapter 18 Authenticated PDF LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ACT Subchapter I. General Provisions Subchapter II. Formation; Certificate of Formation Subchapter III. Members Subchapter IV. Managers Subchapter V. Finance Subchapter VI. Distributions and Resignation Subchapter VII. Assignment of Limited Liability Company Interests Subchapter VIII. Dissolution Subchapter IX. Foreign Limited Liability Companies Subchapter X. Derivative Actions Subchapter XI. Miscellaneous Subchapter XII. Statutory Public Benefit Limited Liability Companies
perturbation.justification: The original contract language states that any conflicting provisions 'shall be deemed...to be modified or altered', creating a firm obligation to conform with applicable law. The changed text weakens this obligation by stating conflicting provisions 'may be subject to modification or alteration', introducing subjectivity and discretion. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguity Legal Contradiction' since it introduces an ambiguous obligation that is difficult to enforce under state or national law, potentially leading to non-compliance. The LLM generated explanation also supports this conclusion.
